new_classes.definition_en: Sparkling wine of fresh grapes, other than Champagne and Prosecco, with an excess pressure of 3 bar or more measured at 20°C, presented in containers holding 2 litres or less, classified under CN subheading 2204 10 96.

coverage_explanation: The legal note for the 22041011–22041098 range refers to Subheading Note 1 of Chapter 22, which defines "sparkling wine" by a minimum excess pressure of 3 bar at 20°C measured in closed containers. The axioms capture: (1) membership in SparklingWine, (2) the presence of an ExcessPressureQuality (with a ≥3 bar datatype range restriction), (3) packaging in a SmallContainer (≤2 L, the container threshold for subheading 2204 10 vs 2204 30), and (4) exclusion of Champagne-region, Prosecco-PDO and Glera-varietal qualities to discriminate the "other" category from the 22041011 (Champagne) and 22041091/92 (Prosecco/Glera) siblings. Coverage is partial because: (a) the exact numeric pressure threshold is attached to the quality class only indirectly via the existing ExcessPressureQuality and excessPressureBar property (no equivalence axiom was possible without a proper intersection in the tool schema); (b) the distinction from other sibling codes within 22041011–22041098 (e.g. 22041093 for Cava, 22041094 for Asti) relies on named geographical/varietal qualities not yet fully established in the running TBox; (c) the note itself is a cross-reference to the subheading note rather than a detailed product description, limiting derivable distinguishing criteria.
